name: Erasca, Inc.

profile.description: Erasca is a clinical-stage precision oncology company focused on discovering, developing, and commercializing therapies for patients with RAS/MAPK pathway-driven cancers. The company's name embodies its mission — erase cancer — and it is advancing a pipeline of novel therapeutic candidates including ERAS-0015 (pan-RAS molecular glue degrader), ERAS-4001 (pan-KRAS inhibitor), and ERAS-12 (EGFR biparatopic antibody), leveraging its proprietary OPRA AI drug discovery platform.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): Founded in July 2018 by CEO Dr. Jonathan Lim, Erasca has advanced multiple clinical candidates targeting the RAS/MAPK pathway but has no approved products or commercial revenue to date [1]. The AURORAS-1 trial for ERAS-0015 showed early confirmed and unconfirmed responses across multiple tumor types and RAS mutations, representing meaningful clinical execution for a clinical-stage biotech but not a transformative commercial launch. AI Deployment (1/2): Erasca developed the proprietary OPRA (Oncology Pattern Recognition Algorithm) platform that uses machine learning to accelerate drug discovery by elucidating novel tumor biology and identifying therapeutic candidates [2]. This is a genuine AI deployment integrated into the company's R&D workflow, though it has not yet produced a commercially validated product or quantifiable revenue impact. Market Share (0/2): Pre-revenue clinical-stage company with no commercial market share. The RAS-targeted oncology space is increasingly competitive with Revolution Medicines, Mirati/BMS (adagrasib), Amgen (sotorasib), and other large pharma players advancing KRAS programs [3]. Pipeline (1/2): Three differentiated clinical candidates — ERAS-0015 (pan-RAS molecular glue degrader), ERAS-4001 (pan-KRAS inhibitor), and ERAS-12 (EGFR biparatopic antibody) — each targeting distinct mechanisms within the RAS/MAPK pathway [1][2]. These are specific and named with general timelines but remain early-to-mid clinical stage without clear registration-enabling trial dates. R&D Intensity (1/1): As a clinical-stage biotech, virtually 100% of Erasca's operational spending is directed toward R&D — Q1 2026 net loss of $183.4M reflects the intensity of clinical investment [4]. This places the company well above industry average for R&D intensity.

inspiration.label: WEAK
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): Erasca's mission is embedded in its name — 'erase cancer' — a portmanteau of 'Eradicate RAS-driven Cancer' [2]. This is specific, bold, and deeply purposeful, connecting the company's precision oncology work directly to a larger aspiration of eliminating cancer in the approximately 5.5 million patients annually affected by RAS/MAPK pathway-driven tumors. CEO Dr. Jonathan Lim, a physician, co-founded the company around this purpose, and the entire pipeline is aligned with it. The mission is specific and thoughtful — clearly differentiated from generic pharma mission statements — but has not yet been validated by decades of commercial execution (founded 2018). Community Impact (0/2): No evidence of significant charitable programs, foundation activity, or community investment beyond the company's clinical mission itself. As a pre-revenue clinical-stage biotech with approximately 200 employees and significant cash burn, philanthropic capacity is limited. Employee Retention (0/2): No publicly available data on employee turnover, average tenure, or retention metrics for Erasca. The company's COO previously co-founded MindScienceConnection focused on building thriving research cultures, suggesting intentionality around team dynamics, but no retention outcomes are quantifiable [1]. Insider Buying (0/2): No specific evidence of meaningful net insider buying by CEO Dr. Lim or other executives in the last 12 months was found. Clinical-stage biotech executives typically hold large equity positions from founding grants rather than demonstrating conviction through recent open-market purchases.

infrastructure.label: WEAK
infrastructure.analysis: Erasca's financial profile reflects a pre-commercial clinical-stage biotech with zero revenue and significant cash burn. Q1 2026 net loss was -$183.4M with EPS of -$0.60; TTM diluted EPS is -$0.69 [4]. The company has approximately $463M in cash, providing runway into the second half of 2027 [1]. With no revenue, metrics such as Forward P/E, PEG ratio, revenue growth YoY, revenue growth 5yr CAGR, and EPS growth are not applicable and are excluded from the calculation. Infrastructure is scored mechanically on available metrics only: ROE is negative (score 0.0, 2x weight) and profit margin is deeply negative with zero revenue (score 0.0, 1x weight). All other growth-stock metrics are null and excluded per the scoring algorithm. Weighted sum = 0.0 / max weighted sum 3.0 = 0, yielding an infrastructure score of 0 [4][5]. Liquidity floor check: 5,190,000 average daily volume x $14.91 = $77.4M daily dollar volume — comfortably above the $1M IOWN liquidity threshold [5].

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (7/9): Erasca's core business — clinical drug development, FDA regulatory processes, and proprietary molecular compound creation — is structurally protected from AI substitution [3]. AI cannot run clinical trials, manufacture drug candidates, navigate multi-year regulatory approval pathways, or replace the physical-world biology of cancer treatment. The company's focus on precision oncology targeting the RAS/MAPK pathway involves deep biological understanding, proprietary molecular design (pan-RAS molecular glues, pan-KRAS inhibitors), and patient-specific treatment strategies that require physical-world execution. The regulatory moat is substantial — even if AI could design a better molecule, it would still need years of clinical trials and FDA review. However, AI-enabled drug discovery platforms (Recursion, Insilico Medicine, and large pharma internal AI efforts) could accelerate competitors' timelines in the same RAS-targeted therapeutic space, partially eroding any first-mover advantage [3]. The clinical development moat remains strong once candidates enter trials. AI Opportunity (0/1): While Erasca's OPRA platform is AI-native and uses machine learning for oncology pattern recognition, the company generates zero revenue — from AI or otherwise [2][4]. The AI platform is an internal R&D tool, not a revenue-generating product. No quantifiable AI revenue exists to cite.

infinite_game.summary: Erasca demonstrates genuine elements of infinite game thinking — the company was founded with a bold Just Cause ('erase cancer') and CEO Dr. Jonathan Lim has dedicated his 35-year career to building biotech companies that advance oncology therapeutics. The CURE values framework (Committed, United, Relentless, Exceptional) and the COO's background in building thriving research cultures signal long-term organizational intent. However, the company is too young (founded 2018) to have been tested through market cycles, leadership transitions, or strategic crises. There is insufficient evidence of existential flexibility or worthy rival recognition to push the score higher.

just_cause.analysis: Erasca's purpose — to erase cancer — meets most of Sinek's criteria for a genuine Just Cause: it is resilient (cancer will always need fighting), inclusive (invites patients, researchers, and clinicians into the mission), service-oriented (benefits humanity beyond shareholders), idealistic (describes a future state that does not yet exist), and big (can never be fully achieved) [2]. CEO Dr. Jonathan Lim, a physician with 35+ years of biotech experience, co-founded the company specifically around the insight that the RAS/MAPK pathway could be drugged, and the entire pipeline targets this pathway in direct service of the cause. The company's name itself IS the mission — a constant reminder of purpose. However, Erasca was founded in 2018 and has not yet proven this cause through a full market cycle, leadership transition, or strategic crisis. The cause is genuine but unvalidated by decades of execution. Score: 2/3 weighted points.

trusting_teams.analysis: Limited public data is available on Erasca's internal culture. The company's COO previously co-founded MindScienceConnection, an organization focused on building thriving research cultures, which suggests deliberate intentionality around team dynamics and psychological safety [1]. CEO Dr. Lim's track record of leading six biotech companies with seven product approvals implies organizational capability in attracting and retaining scientific talent. The CURE values framework (Committed, United, Relentless, Exceptional) signals cultural intentionality. However, at approximately 200 employees, the organizational structure provides limited public visibility into trust dynamics. No employee retention data, Glassdoor reviews, or satisfaction metrics are publicly available. Score: 1/2 weighted points.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Erasca operates in the increasingly competitive RAS-targeted oncology space alongside Revolution Medicines, Bristol-Myers Squibb (via Mirati acquisition with adagrasib), Amgen (sotorasib), and multiple large pharma companies developing KRAS inhibitors [3]. However, there is no public evidence that Erasca's leadership frames these competitors as worthy rivals in Sinek's sense — companies whose strengths reveal Erasca's own weaknesses and push it to improve. Competitive language in investor presentations focuses on Erasca's differentiated mechanisms ('best-in-class,' 'first-in-class pan-RAS') rather than on what can be learned from rivals' clinical successes. This is a finite competitive framing. Score: 0/1 weighted point.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Erasca was founded in 2018 and has not yet faced the kind of strategic test that reveals existential flexibility in Sinek's framework. The company has pursued its original RAS/MAPK pathway focus consistently since founding, which demonstrates commitment to the Just Cause but has not required a painful strategic pivot or abandonment of a profitable business line. There is no profitable business to sacrifice — the company is pre-revenue. While Erasca has made portfolio prioritization decisions (focusing resources on lead programs), these are standard clinical-stage capital allocation choices, not transformative reinventions. The company is too young and too early-stage to have encountered the kind of existential disruption that tests this dimension. Score: 0/2 weighted points.

courage_to_lead.analysis: CEO Dr. Jonathan Lim brings 35+ years of biotechnology experience and has been chairman or CEO of six biotech companies that collectively achieved seven FDA product approvals and generated approximately $10 billion in shareholder value [1]. His decision to co-found Erasca around the historically 'undruggable' RAS target — after a career where he could have pursued lower-risk opportunities — demonstrates meaningful conviction. The company has invested heavily in R&D with a Q1 2026 net loss of $183.4M, maintaining focus on the long-term mission of erasing cancer rather than pivoting to nearer-term revenue opportunities [4]. However, clinical-stage biotech spending is somewhat expected rather than a courageous deviation from norms, and there is no example of sacrificing an existing profitable business for a riskier but mission-aligned path. Score: 1/2 weighted points.

investment_thesis: Erasca offers high-risk, high-reward exposure to precision oncology targeting the RAS/MAPK pathway — the most commonly altered signaling pathway in cancer, present in approximately 30% of all human cancers and affecting 5.5 million patients annually worldwide [2]. The $4.68B market cap reflects significant pipeline optionality across three differentiated clinical candidates: ERAS-0015 (pan-RAS molecular glue degrader), ERAS-4001 (potential first-in-class pan-KRAS inhibitor), and ERAS-12 (EGFR biparatopic antibody) [1]. CEO Dr. Jonathan Lim's track record — 35+ years of biotech leadership, six companies, seven FDA product approvals, and approximately $10B in shareholder value creation — provides experienced clinical-stage navigation [1]. The proprietary OPRA AI drug discovery platform adds a technology edge in candidate identification [2]. With $463M in cash providing runway into H2 2027 and average daily dollar volume of $77M, the stock offers sufficient liquidity for institutional positioning [4][5]. Faith Alignment is strong at +38 (ALIGNED) across five positive Inspire attributions including Product Access and Affordability.
thesis_continued: The bear case is severe and reflects the fundamental reality of pre-commercial biotech investing. Erasca has zero revenue, burned $183.4M in Q1 2026 alone (EPS -$0.60), and the $4.68B market cap prices in substantial clinical success that remains entirely unproven [4]. The $463M cash runway into H2 2027 means the company will likely need additional capital — potentially at dilutive terms — before any product reaches commercialization, which typically requires 3-5+ additional years from early clinical stages. Infrastructure scores 0/10 due to the complete absence of revenue, negative earnings, and negative ROE — this is a pre-commercial entity entirely dependent on binary clinical outcomes. The RAS-targeted oncology space has become a competitive battlefield, with Revolution Medicines, Bristol-Myers Squibb (Mirati acquisition/adagrasib), Amgen (sotorasib), and multiple large pharma entrants advancing their own programs with deeper resources and later-stage data [3]. A single pivotal trial failure could destroy the thesis. The overall IOWN score of 39 (SELL) reflects not a judgment on the science, but the structural reality that pre-revenue clinical-stage companies cannot score well on financial Infrastructure, creating an inherent disadvantage in the IOWN framework regardless of pipeline quality.
key_catalysts: ERAS-0015 Phase 1 monotherapy data readouts — positive efficacy and safety signals in pan-RAS molecular glue degrader program could validate the mechanism and drive significant re-rating given the large addressable market of RAS-mutant tumors [1][2]

ERAS-4001 clinical data as a potential first-in-class pan-KRAS inhibitor — differentiated from existing single-mutation inhibitors (sotorasib, adagrasib) by targeting multiple KRAS mutations simultaneously [1]

Potential partnership or licensing deals with large pharma for pipeline candidates — Erasca's differentiated pan-RAS mechanisms could attract strategic interest from companies seeking broader RAS coverage than single-mutation approaches [3]
key_risks: Binary clinical trial risk — early Phase 1 dose-escalation responses may not translate to meaningful efficacy in expansion cohorts, and the pan-RAS molecular glue approach remains novel and unproven at pivotal scale [1][2]

Cash burn of $183.4M in Q1 2026 against $463M cash creates approximately 5-6 quarters of runway into H2 2027, after which dilutive capital raises are likely needed to fund continued clinical development through registration [4]

Intensifying competition in RAS-targeted oncology from Revolution Medicines, BMS/Mirati (adagrasib), Amgen (sotorasib), and emerging biotechs with deeper financial resources and more advanced clinical programs could erode Erasca's differentiation and limit commercial potential [3]
